title: Qlog Main Schema Parity Architecture
purpose: Record how the repository will close the remaining qlog main-schema guidance that was outside the original contained and sequential writer baseline.
design_summary: Keep already-supported main-schema behavior in the existing retained model, JSON serializers, importer, and QUIC raw-info helpers, while treating event-importance metadata, QLOGDIR-driven generation, and security-sensitive capture policy as follow-up requirements that must not be implied complete by the older baseline artifacts.
key_components: Incursa.Qlog retained model and JSON parser
Incursa.Qlog contained and sequential JSON serializers
data_and_state_considerations: The retained model should continue to preserve unknown qlog data without requiring type-specific schema knowledge. Main-schema parity should add metadata and generation policy only where the repository has a real surface for it, keeping operator security policy explicit instead of silently broadening the serializer contract.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The official QUICWG page is a latest draft preview, while the repository still carries a local draft-13 text snapshot for the original baseline.
Event importance is schema metadata and should not be smuggled into individual event payloads.
QLOGDIR and QLOGFILE are generation controls, not serializer parameters.
Security, privacy, retention, and encryption policies cross into application and operator boundaries.
risks: Treating planned guidelines as implemented would overstate conformance.
Adding environment-variable behavior directly to low-level serializers would make pure serialization unexpectedly stateful.
Ignoring event-importance metadata would leave later generated protocol schema surfaces incomplete.
open_questions: Should event-importance metadata live in a reusable schema-description model or remain authored in SpecTrace until a public schema API exists?
Should QLOGFILE support be first-class or remain optional behind QLOGDIR-based generation?
